title: x402 Implementation: Securing Coolify and Hetzner with Micro-Stakes
excerpt: A technical deep dive into the Coolify and Hetzner deployment surface that runs Veklom's VNP telemetry, x402, and control-plane routes.
content:
As enterprise AI routing becomes mission-critical, the deployment substrate must be measurable, governed, and financially constrained. In this post, we analyze the Coolify and Hetzner surface that runs Veklom's VNP telemetry through the lens of the Veklom Nexus Protocol.

### The Architecture Challenge

When autonomous agents interact with production APIs, they generate highly unpredictable access patterns. Traditional security and monitoring tools rely on human-speed assumptions. A physical control plane running through Coolify, Traefik, Docker, Postgres, Redis, and Hetzner nodes needs deterministic policy, payment, and topology evidence rather than provider-controlled status claims.

### Integrating with VNP

By mapping the Coolify and Hetzner deployment surface into the VNP Methodology v1.0 verification stack, Veklom observes the exact operational signals that matter: physical measurements, signed telemetry, route beacons, x402 settlement evidence, PGL audit trails, and agent/runtime enforcement.

```mermaid
pie title "Coolify and Hetzner Failure Modes"
    "Timeout (TTFB)" : 45
    "Container or route restart" : 25
    "TLS handshake jitter" : 20
    "DNS or proxy routing" : 10
```

Through VNP telemetry and x402 settlement evidence, Veklom can bind API route usage to receipts, live topology state, and backend-enforced payment configuration instead of publishing unsupported live claims.

### The Path Forward

The Veklom control plane is deployed through Coolify on Hetzner, with public traffic routed through Cloudflare and Traefik into the production containers. VNP grades that real substrate using VNP Methodology v1.0 and exposes status labels from backend evidence, not from marketing copy.
